--- title: "[pairwise_logrank_test()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Pairwise log-rank tests for all group pairs with multiple-comparison correction. ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/_tests.py#L431-L605){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python pairwise_logrank_test( surv, group, *, rho=0.0, gamma=0.0, strata=None, correction="holm", format=None ) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Runs the log-rank test on every pair of groups, then adjusts p-values to control for multiple testing. This answers the question: "Which pairs of groups have significantly different survival?" when you have more than two groups. After the global log-rank test (via `logrank_test`) indicates groups differ, this pairwise test reveals which pairs are significantly different and by how much. P-values are adjusted across all pairs using a chosen correction method to control the false discovery rate or family-wise error rate. **Typical workflow**: First run `logrank_test` to test overall group differences. If significant, use `pairwise_logrank_test` to identify which pairs differ. The adjusted p-values account for testing multiple pairs from the same data. ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [surv]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Surv]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A `Surv` response object representing censored survival times. Supports right-censored data or counting-process format. Constructed with `Surv.right()`, `Surv.counting()`, or `Surv.multistate()`. [group]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : Group labels, one per observation. Can be a Narwhals series, 1-D array, or Python sequence. Must have at least 3 unique levels (to create multiple pairs). Must have the same length as `surv`. [rho]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[0.0]{.fl}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Fleming-Harrington weight exponents for the log-rank test (same as `logrank_test`). Default `(0, 0)` gives standard log-rank; `(1, 0)` gives Peto-Peto (emphasizes early events). [gamma]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[0.0]{.fl}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Fleming-Harrington weight exponents for the log-rank test (same as `logrank_test`). Default `(0, 0)` gives standard log-rank; `(1, 0)` gives Peto-Peto (emphasizes early events). [strata]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Optional stratifying factor. When provided, each pairwise test is stratified by this factor (computed within each stratum, then combined). Use to control for confounding. [correction]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [["holm"]{.st}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Multiple-comparison adjustment method applied across all pairwise p-values: - `"holm"` (default): Controls family-wise error rate. Conservative; recommended for small numbers of pairs (fewer than ~10). - `"bh"`: Benjamini-Hochberg false-discovery rate. Less conservative; recommended for many pairs. Allows more false positives but focuses on their rate. - `"bonferroni"`: Bonferroni correction. Very conservative; adjusted $p = p \times m$, where $m$ is the number of pairs. - `"none"`: No adjustment. Use only if you're testing a single pre-planned pair (though use `logrank_test` directly in that case). [format]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str | None]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Output format: `None` (default), `"pandas"`, `"polars"`, or `"pyarrow"`. When `None`, a backend is auto-detected (Polars, then Pandas, then PyArrow). ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [pandas.DataFrame, polars.DataFrame, or pyarrow.Table]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : One row per pair of groups with columns: - `group1`, `group2`: The pair of group labels being compared. - `statistic`: Chi-square test statistic for the pair. - `p_value`: Raw (unadjusted) log-rank p-value for the pair. - `p_adjusted`: Adjusted p-value after multiple-comparison correction. Use this for significance testing (e.g., p_adjusted < 0.05). ::: ## Details {.doc-details} The number of pairs tested is $C(k, 2) = k(k-1)/2$, where $k$ is the number of groups. For $k=3$, that's 3 pairs; for $k=5$, that's 10 pairs. Larger numbers of pairs can reduce power per comparison (wider adjusted confidence intervals), so keep the number of groups reasonable when possible. The adjustment method affects stringency: Holm controls false discovery more strictly (lower type-I error, higher type-II error), while Benjamini-Hochberg is more permissive (higher type-I error rate overall, but controls the proportion of false discoveries). ## Examples {.doc-examples} Test pairwise survival differences among the four cell types in the `veteran` dataset. A global log-rank test first shows that cell types differ overall, but doesn't say which pairs differ: ::: {#b52db05b .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw # Load data and build a right-censored response vet = gw.load_dataset("veteran", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(vet["time"], event=vet["status"]) # Run a global log-rank test across all cell types gw.logrank_test(y, group=vet["celltype"]) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` TestResult(method='Log-rank test', statistic=25.4037, df=3, p_value=1.271e-05) ``` ::: ::: The pairwise test compares all six pairs of cell types and returns a table with the test statistic, raw p-value, and adjusted p-value for each pair. Pass `format=` to choose the backend (here, Polars); use `p_adjusted` for significance testing: ::: {#1cb0ab68 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Compare all pairs of cell types with Holm-adjusted p-values pairs = gw.pairwise_logrank_test(y, group=vet["celltype"], format="polars") pairs ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
shape: (6, 5)
group1group2statisticp_valuep_adjusted
strstrf64f64f64
"adeno""large"17.6693220.0000260.000158
"adeno""smallcell"0.0968430.7556510.755651
"adeno""squamous"12.0454840.0005190.002596
"large""smallcell"9.3709040.0022050.006614
"large""squamous"0.8225940.3644230.728846
"smallcell""squamous"11.5736740.0006690.002676
``` ::: ::: Filter to significant pairs (adjusted p-value < 0.05). Request `format="pandas"` here so we can use boolean-mask filtering: ::: {#c6676a60 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Filter to pairs with statistically significant differences pairs = gw.pairwise_logrank_test(y, group=vet["celltype"], format="pandas") pairs[pairs["p_adjusted"] < 0.05] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
group1 group2 statistic p_value p_adjusted
0 adeno large 17.669322 0.000026 0.000158
2 adeno squamous 12.045484 0.000519 0.002596
3 large smallcell 9.370904 0.002205 0.006614
5 smallcell squamous 11.573674 0.000669 0.002676
``` ::: ::: Use the Peto-Peto (Wilcoxon) weighting to emphasize early survival differences: ::: {#9aa15ee2 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Use Peto-Peto weighting to emphasize early event times gw.pairwise_logrank_test(y, group=vet["celltype"], rho=1, format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
shape: (6, 5)
group1group2statisticp_valuep_adjusted
strstrf64f64f64
"adeno""large"13.8317370.00020.0012
"adeno""smallcell"0.0418610.8378841.0
"adeno""squamous"6.4849050.0108790.032638
"large""smallcell"12.2927520.0004550.002274
"large""squamous"0.0214840.8834691.0
"smallcell""squamous"7.8879250.0049770.019906
``` ::: ::: Use Benjamini-Hochberg adjustment (less conservative) if you're interested in which pairs show evidence of differences (false-discovery rate control rather than family-wise error): ::: {#7acf5f0c .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Use Benjamini-Hochberg correction for false-discovery rate control gw.pairwise_logrank_test(y, group=vet["celltype"], correction="bh", format="polars") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
shape: (6, 5)
group1group2statisticp_valuep_adjusted
strstrf64f64f64
"adeno""large"17.6693220.0000260.000158
"adeno""smallcell"0.0968430.7556510.755651
"adeno""squamous"12.0454840.0005190.001338
"large""smallcell"9.3709040.0022050.003307
"large""squamous"0.8225940.3644230.437307
"smallcell""squamous"11.5736740.0006690.001338
``` ::: :::
